meaning: n.浠庡墠瑗跨彮鐗欓噾甯佺殑鍚嶇О
examples: To lie on the bottom of the sky, like a gold doubloon.
If this pattern continues for another 40 years, a gold doubloon will buy about what it does today.
Be warned: this pillage has a two-hour, thirty-minute running time, but I still say the pirate sequel will make many a doubloon.
